title: Patent Amortization
event: Hollis Co. purchases a patent for $480,000. Its remaining legal life is 20 years, but Hollis expects the technology to be useful for only 8 years. Prepare the annual amortization entry.

lines.label: 480,000 ÷ 8
lines.why: Amortize over the SHORTER of legal life and useful life — the economics beat the paperwork.
lines.trap: 20-year legal life is the distractor: usefulness runs out first, so 8 years governs.

lines.label: asset reduced directly
lines.why: Intangibles are typically credited directly, no accumulated account.
lines.trap: A successful legal DEFENSE cost is capitalized into the patent; an unsuccessful one is expensed — and may signal impairment.

memorize.body: Amortization = cost ÷ shorter of legal life or useful life. Straight-line, usually no salvage.
Limited-life intangibles amortize; INDEFINITE-life intangibles (goodwill, most trademarks) don't — they get impairment tests instead.

questions.prompt: Patent $480,000, legal life 20 years, useful life 8 years. Annual amortization = ?

distractors.feedback: You used the 20-year LEGAL life. Useful life is shorter and governs: 480,000 ÷ 8 = 60,000.
No-amortization is the INDEFINITE-life treatment (goodwill). Patents have limited lives — amortize over 8 years.

build.scaffold: Shorter life wins. Debit the expense, credit the patent directly.
